lesson.menu_title: Reaching Agreement -- Confirming Terms & Closing Strongly
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Reaching Agreement -- Confirming Terms & Closing Strongly
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Natalie Viegas | Aula 20 -- Reaching Agreement

listenings.text: Hi Natalie, Tom here, just before you confirm the deal with the client. A quick word on closing strongly. You have done the hard part, you reached an agreement. Now you have to close it cleanly. First, restate every term out loud and confirm it, so nothing is left open. Then put it in writing, the same day if you can, because a deal is only real when it is on paper. When you talk about timing, project the finished result. Use the future perfect. Say something like, by Friday, we will have signed everything, or by tonight, I will have sent the terms in writing. It sounds confident and it sets a clear deadline. And remember, the best deals are mutually beneficial, good for both sides, so you can both move forward and seal the deal with a smile. You have come a long way. Go and close it. Talk soon.

Hi, this is Natalie, recording a quick note after I sealed the deal with the client. And it is also my last note of the whole program. To close it, I followed Tom's advice. I restated every term and confirmed it, so nothing was left open. We locked in the rate for two years, and we only had to iron out the payment dates. Then I projected the finished result with the future perfect. I told the client that by tonight I would have sent the terms in writing, and by Friday we will have signed the contract. It sounded confident and clear. The deal is mutually beneficial, so we both felt good about it, and we agreed to move forward with the rollout. I will touch base with them next week. Honestly, I feel proud. I have come a long way since lesson one. I can now run a meeting, present, negotiate, stand my ground, and close a deal in English. That feels amazing.

extra_audio.text: First, you sum up the discussion so both sides are on the same page. Then you restate each term and confirm it out loud, so nothing is left open. After that, you lock in what is agreed and iron out the last small details. Next, you project the finished result with the future perfect, saying that by Friday you will have signed the contract. Finally, you put everything in writing, seal the deal, and move forward.
